name: Grok 4.3 (High)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (size undisclosed); high reasoning-effort configuration of the same served weights as Grok 4.3
note: High reasoning-effort tier of Grok 4.3, same underlying weights as the default/low-effort configuration, opted into via the API's reasoning_effort parameter. This is the configuration Artificial Analysis benchmarks by default for Grok 4.3.

results.notes: GPQA Diamond, as measured/reported by Artificial Analysis; high reasoning-effort configuration. Evaluated at launch, 2026-04-30.
tau2-Bench Telecom, as measured by Artificial Analysis at launch, 2026-04-30 (also cited as 98% by other outlets).

Artificial Analysis's own launch article for Grok 4.3 states 'GDPval-AA: 1500 ELO (up 321 points from Grok 4.20)'. Cross-corroborated verbatim by officechai.com ('Grok 4.3 Scores 53 On Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index...') and progressiverobot.com's Grok 4.3 review. AA benchmarks the high reasoning-effort configuration by default for this model family (per this entry's existing note), consistent with how the pre-existing gpqa/tau2-bench-telecom scores were attributed here. [score corrected during round-5 full-corpus accuracy re-verify: 1085 -> 1500]

Corroborated across 3 independent sources: Artificial Analysis's own account on X ('...ranks top on the IFBench leaderboard 81% for precise instruction-following on Artificial Analysis'), officechai.com, and kilo.ai's Grok 4.3 coding benchmark page. A later AA leaderboard snapshot (fetched 2026-07-14) shows a slightly revised 81.3%, suggesting a minor point-update since launch; used the launch-day 81% figure that all initial sources agree on.

Humanity's Last Exam, as measured by Artificial Analysis (high reasoning-effort configuration). Previously flagged in this file's own verificationNotes as single-sourced (TheAIRankings only) and omitted; a fresh re-fetch of TheAIRankings still shows 35.0%, and it is now independently corroborated by a second, differently-styled aggregator, benchlm.ai ('AA-HLE: 35.0%', exact match), which clears the two-source bar this file applies elsewhere. TheAIRankings itself notes xAI 'did not publish a full first-party benchmark table for Grok 4.3' and explicitly lists SWE-bench Verified/Pro, AIME 2025, and MMLU as 'data not available' for this model -- searched specifically for these and could not find a reliable independently-published figure for any of them (one source, requesty.ai, noted SWE-bench/ARC-AGI-2 cells were left intentionally blank because 'older Grok 4/4.20 scores are sometimes recycled incorrectly' for Grok 4.3, which is a useful caution against forcing a match).

Agents' Last Exam (ALE-CLI, Overall leaderboard), OpenClaw harness, High reasoning effort: Pass rate 4.3% / Score 15.5% (rank 50, listed as 'grok-4-3' + High). Matches this catalog entry's own effortLabel ('high'), distinguishing it from the plain 'Grok 4.3' entry/row. Score component used per corpus convention.

verificationNotes:
GPQA Diamond 90.1% corroborated across 3 independent write-ups summarizing Artificial Analysis's Grok 4.3 model page (TheAIRankings, Requesty, FelloAI). tau2-bench-telecom ~97.7-98% corroborated by both Artificial Analysis's own launch article and TheAIRankings. I deliberately dropped several numbers I could not corroborate: a composite 'AA Intelligence Index' figure that came back inconsistently across fetches (53.2, 53, 38, and 37.6 in different sources/pages — likely different sub-metrics or fetch-summarizer noise) is omitted since it has no matching benchmark slug in the valid enum anyway; a Humanity's Last Exam score of 35.0% appeared in only one source (TheAIRankings) and a follow-up targeted search could not corroborate it (only found Grok 4 / Grok 4 Heavy HLE numbers), so it is omitted per the 'omit rather than guess' rule.

SUPPLEMENTARY COVERAGE PASS (2026-07-14): Re-searched specifically for harder/agentic evals missing from this thin (4-result) entry -- SWE-bench, LiveCodeBench, Terminal-Bench, AIME, MMLU-Pro, MMMU-Pro. Multiple independent write-ups (theairankings.com, frankx.ai, requesty.ai) explicitly confirm xAI never published a first-party benchmark table for Grok 4.3 and that SWE-bench Verified/Pro, AIME, ARC-AGI-2, and MMLU have no reliable independently-published score for this specific model -- one source flagged that older Grok 4/4.20 SWE-bench numbers get recycled/misattributed to 4.3, reinforcing the decision not to force a match. This pass did clear the corroboration bar on Humanity's Last Exam (35.0%, added above) once a second aggregator matched TheAIRankings' figure exactly. MMMU-Pro (78.1%) and several 'AA-' branded composite/agentic metrics (SciCode, Terminal-Bench Hard, AA Coding/Agentic/Omniscience indices, GDPval-AA normalized, Gert Labs Composite, ResearchClawBench, APEX-Agents-AA, AA Briefcase, EnterpriseOps-Gym, ITBench) appeared on benchlm.ai but had no second corroborating source and/or no matching taxonomy slug, so were omitted per this file's own 'omit rather than guess' precedent.

CORE-BENCHMARK GAP-FILL PASS (2026-07-16): Directly fetched Vals AI's Grok 4.3 model page (vals.ai/models/grok_grok-4.3), confirming its 'Reasoning Effort' field reads 'high' -- matching this entry's configuration exactly. Vals AI's percentage figures render via a client-side progress-bar animation that shows a static '0.0%' placeholder in a plain fetch/summary; the true scores were recovered from the raw page markup's bar-fill values and cross-checked against the displayed rank fractions (e.g. '23/82') for consistency. This surfaced three previously-missing core benchmarks: LiveCodeBench (84.49%, rank 27/127), SWE-bench Verified (71.4%, rank 41/70, filed under the base 'swe-bench' slug per corpus convention), and MMMU (83.06%, rank 23/82) -- all added above. For the remaining four core gaps (MMLU, MATH, AIME, HumanEval), the same Vals AI page has no rows for any of them (it tracks MMLU Pro -- a distinct benchmark/slug -- instead of base MMLU, and has no MATH/AIME/HumanEval rows at all); Epoch AI has not evaluated any Grok model past Grok 4.1 Fast (confirmed via direct search of epoch.ai/models -- no Grok 4.3 entry exists there at all); and a direct fetch of benchlm.ai's Grok 4.3 page explicitly states Grok 4.3 does 'not directly report' AIME 2025 and lists no MMLU, MATH, or HumanEval figure. This corroborates and extends the prior-round conclusion (already in this file) that xAI never published a first-party benchmark table for Grok 4.3 covering these four legacy academic evals, and matches the identical finding independently reached for the newer sibling Grok 4.5 (2026-07-14 pass: 'No academic/knowledge benchmarks (MMLU, MMLU-Pro, AIME, MATH, GSM8K...) have been published by xAI or any independent tracker'). Treating mmlu, math, aime, and humaneval as omitted_no_evidence -- consistent with the broader pattern of frontier labs no longer publishing saturated legacy academic benchmarks for new flagship releases, not a research gap on my part.

SECOND-PASS LEGACY-BENCHMARK SEARCH VIA NEW SOURCE TYPES (2026-07-17): Ran a targeted follow-up search for mmlu/math/aime/humaneval using only source types the prior round had NOT already exhausted (that round covered official launch materials, Epoch AI, Vals.ai, LMArena, BenchLM, llm-stats.com). Checked all 6 newly-specified source types:

(1) Stanford HELM -- fetched crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite/latest/ (Core scenarios, which include an MMLU column and a MATH column) and the HELM Capabilities leaderboard directly via a real browser render (not a static HTML fetch, since both pages are JS-rendered SPAs). Read the full leaderboard tables. Neither leaderboard contains any Grok 4.3 row at all: HELM Capabilities' most recent xAI entry is "Grok 4 (0709)" (mean score 0.785, MMLU-Pro 0.851, Omni-MATH 0.603 -- a different model, not 4.3), and HELM Lite's Core-scenarios leaderboard (which has the actual MMLU-EM and MATH-Equivalent-CoT columns) contains zero Grok models of any version in its ~90 listed rows. This is a confirmed absence, not merely non-corroboration -- Stanford CRFM simply has not run Grok 4.3 through HELM.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar -- searched multiple phrasings ("Grok 4.3 MMLU site:arxiv.org", "Grok 4.3 MATH benchmark arxiv paper baseline comparison", direct semanticscholar.org query). Found exactly one paper using "Grok-4.3" as an evaluated model: arxiv.org/abs/2511.13725 ("KILLBENCH: A Benchmark for External AI Kill Switch Feasibility"), which evaluates Grok-4.3 alongside GPT-5.2, Gemma4, Qwen3.6 and Qwen3.5-uncensored -- but only on kill-switch-defense-method metrics; it reports no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers for any model in it. No other arXiv or Semantic Scholar paper surfaced with a baseline table reporting any of these four scores for this model.

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs -- searched for lm-evaluation-harness results.json / results/ directories referencing "grok-4-3" or "grok_4.3"; GitHub's own topic page for "grok-4-3" has zero tagged repositories. Directly inspected groq/openbench (an eval-harness/infra repo that surfaced in search) -- it contains no reference to Grok 4.3 anywhere. No committed run logs against this model were found.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators -- LiveBench.ai: confirmed (via direct browser read of the live leaderboard) a "Grok 4.3" row -- Overall 62.3, Reasoning 70.8, Coding 69.9, Agentic Coding 18.5, Mathematics 84.3, Data Analysis 55.8, Language 73.6, Instruction Following 62.8, cost $0.061/successful task -- but LiveBench's "Mathematics" (and other) categories are its own proprietary, contamination-free task sets refreshed every 6 months, not the standard MMLU / MATH-500 / AIME / HumanEval datasets this corpus's mmlu/math/aime/humaneval slugs specifically track (MATH-500 per this corpus's own benchmarks.json), so none of these cells can be honestly mapped to those slugs -- consistent with this file's existing precedent of not force-matching differently-scoped composite metrics onto specific taxonomy slugs. OpenRouter's model page (openrouter.ai/x-ai/grok-4.3) "Benchmarks" section explicitly states "Metrics sourced from Artificial Analysis" (the same source already exhausted) and lists only GPQA/HLE/IFBench/tau2-Bench/AA-LCR/GDPval-AA/CritPt/SciCode/Terminal-Bench-Hard/AA-Omniscience -- no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. Also directly checked Scale AI SEAL/Showdown leaderboards, PapersWithCode, lmcouncil.ai, tokencalculator.com, and requesty.ai's Grok 4.3 page: none list a populated MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval score for Grok 4.3 -- lmcouncil.ai and tokencalculator.com don't carry a Grok 4.3 entry at all yet (tokencalculator.com's only xAI row is "Grok 3.5"), and requesty.ai's own benchmark chart explicitly references "MMLU Pro, GPQA Diamond, SWE-Bench Verified, HumanEval, MATH, AIME, MMMU, and LiveBench" as tracked categories but leaves the MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval cells blank for this model.

(5) Non-English-language sources -- ran Japanese/Chinese/Korean-mixed queries combining "Grok 4.3" with MMLU/benchmark terms; results were limited to generic Grok-3/Grok-4 explainer blogs (aismiley.co.jp, note.com, zhuanlan.zhihu.com) with no independently-run Grok 4.3 MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figures.

(6) Sibling models -- checked Grok 4.5's launch coverage (which, per this file's existing 2026-07-14 note, likewise lacks these four benchmarks) and OpenRouter's "More models from xAI" comparison section for any table that uses Grok 4.3 as a baseline/comparison point with an MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval figure; found none.

Conclusion: having exhausted all 6 newly-specified source types (not merely the minimum 3), no credible, citable MMLU, MATH-500, AIME, or HumanEval score exists for Grok 4.3 (High) in any independent academic evaluation, tracker, leaderboard, or eval-harness log as of 2026-07-17. Re-confirming (not re-guessing) omitted_no_evidence for all four slugs -- this reads as a genuine, persistent absence of independently-published legacy-academic-benchmark data for this specific model/configuration, matching the pattern already documented for its Grok 4.5 sibling, rather than a research shortfall in this pass.
